title: Document approval workflow for regulatory submissions
summary: STRUCTURAL EXAMPLE — process slice type. This file demonstrates the structural shape of a process slice, including at least three ordered steps each with per-step failure behavior, a terminal step, and process-level business rules. The domain content is fictional and must not be copied.

stakeholder_intent.actor: Document author submitting a regulatory document for internal approval
stakeholder_intent.goal: Advance a draft document through a structured multi-step approval workflow until it reaches a final APPROVED or REJECTED status
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Replaces an ad-hoc email approval chain with an auditable, automated workflow that enforces department and compliance constraints
stakeholder_intent.trigger: A document author advances the workflow by calling the approval endpoint at each step
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: The document passes manager review and compliance sign-off and is marked APPROVED in the approval registry

in_scope: Three-step approval workflow: submission, manager review, compliance sign-off
Per-step authorization checks (author for submission, same-department manager for review, certified compliance officer for sign-off)
Recording approval decisions and timestamps at each step
out_of_scope: External regulatory filing after internal approval
Automated escalation if a reviewer is inactive

preconditions: The document exists and its status is DRAFT
The requestor is an authenticated internal employee
The document has at least one line item so completeness validation can succeed

business_rules.rule: Only the document author may initiate the submission step
business_rules.applies_when: The advance endpoint is called with action SUBMIT
business_rules.outcome: Non-authors receive UNAUTHORIZED_SUBMITTER; the document remains in DRAFT

business_rules.rule: The reviewing manager must belong to the same department as the document's owning team
business_rules.applies_when: The advance endpoint is called with action APPROVE or REJECT at the manager review step
business_rules.outcome: Cross-department managers receive UNAUTHORIZED_REVIEWER; the document remains in PENDING_REVIEW

business_rules.rule: Compliance sign-off is mandatory for all documents classified as REGULATORY before they may reach APPROVED status
business_rules.applies_when: The advance endpoint is called with action CERTIFY or REJECT at the compliance step
business_rules.outcome: Only a certified compliance officer may finalize REGULATORY documents; others receive UNAUTHORIZED_COMPLIANCE_OFFICER

inputs.name: approvalId
constraints: Must identify an existing approval record

inputs.name: action
constraints: Enum: SUBMIT, APPROVE, REJECT, CERTIFY; must be valid for the current step

inputs.name: comment
constraints: Max 2000 characters; required when action is REJECT

outputs.body: JSON object with approvalId, currentStep, status, and advancedAt timestamp

errors.condition: Requestor is not authorized for the current step
errors.message: The requestor does not meet the authorization requirement for this workflow step

errors.condition: Action is not valid for the document's current step
errors.message: The requested action cannot be applied at the current workflow step

process_steps.name: Submission
process_steps.trigger: Document author calls the advance endpoint with action SUBMIT
process_steps.action: Validate document completeness (at least one line item present) and transition status from DRAFT to PENDING_REVIEW
process_steps.outcome: Document is queued for manager review and the assigned manager is notified
process_steps.failure_behavior: If the author check fails, return UNAUTHORIZED_FOR_STEP and keep status as DRAFT; if completeness validation fails, return DOCUMENT_INCOMPLETE and keep status as DRAFT

process_steps.name: Manager review
process_steps.trigger: Same-department manager calls the advance endpoint with action APPROVE or REJECT
process_steps.action: Record the manager's decision and timestamp; on APPROVE transition to PENDING_COMPLIANCE; on REJECT transition to REJECTED
process_steps.state_after: PENDING_COMPLIANCE on approval, REJECTED on rejection
process_steps.outcome: Document proceeds to compliance sign-off or is permanently rejected with a mandatory comment
process_steps.failure_behavior: If the manager is from a different department, return UNAUTHORIZED_FOR_STEP and keep status as PENDING_REVIEW

process_steps.name: Compliance sign-off
process_steps.trigger: Certified compliance officer calls the advance endpoint with action CERTIFY or REJECT
process_steps.action: Record the compliance decision and timestamp; on CERTIFY transition to APPROVED; on REJECT transition to REJECTED
process_steps.state_after: APPROVED on certification, REJECTED on rejection
process_steps.outcome: Approval workflow is complete; the document is either approved for regulatory submission or permanently rejected
process_steps.failure_behavior: If the requestor does not hold the COMPLIANCE_OFFICER role, return UNAUTHORIZED_FOR_STEP and keep status as PENDING_COMPLIANCE

security.authentication: Validate the employee session token from the Authorization header using the existing EmployeeAuthMiddleware
authorization_rules: Step 1 (SUBMIT): requestor must be the document author
Step 2 (APPROVE/REJECT): requestor must be a manager in the same department as the document's owning team
Step 3 (CERTIFY/REJECT): requestor must hold the COMPLIANCE_OFFICER role
security.data_classification: Internal confidential; approval records include document metadata and reviewer identities
audit_requirements: Record actor id, approval id, step name, action, and timestamp for every advance operation

architecture_constraints: HTTP handlers call application use cases and never access repositories directly
Each step's authorization check runs before any state mutation
allowed_changes: Add the advance-workflow use case, HTTP handler, step authorization guards, and unit tests
forbidden_changes: Do not modify document content or versioning logic
Do not bypass step ordering; steps must execute in PS-001, PS-002, PS-003 sequence
dependencies: Reuse the existing NotificationService from the HR module for reviewer notifications
implementation_context.migration_strategy: Add approval_step and compliance_decision columns to the approvals table; both are nullable to support in-progress records
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Lock the approval record within the step transition transaction to prevent concurrent advances; reject a second advance if the status has already changed

acceptance_scenarios.title: Document author advances through all three steps to approval
given: A DRAFT document exists with at least one line item
The requestor is the document author
A certified compliance officer is available
when: The author calls advance with action SUBMIT
The manager calls advance with action APPROVE
The compliance officer calls advance with action CERTIFY
then: After SUBMIT the document status is PENDING_REVIEW
After APPROVE the document status is PENDING_COMPLIANCE
After CERTIFY the document status is APPROVED
Each step records the actor id and timestamp
All three advance calls return HTTP 200

acceptance_scenarios.title: Cross-department manager is rejected at the review step
given: The requestor is a manager from a different department
when: The manager calls advance with action APPROVE
then: The response is HTTP 403 with code UNAUTHORIZED_FOR_STEP

test_cases: Unit-test each step's authorization guard with authorized and unauthorized actors
Unit-test invalid action rejection at each step
Integration-test the full three-step happy path against the approval repository
non_functional_requirements: Each advance operation must complete within 500 ms including database writes
definition_of_done: Audit log entries are written for every advance operation

decision_log.decision: Enforce step ordering by checking the current approval status before allowing each advance action
decision_log.rationale: The existing approval state machine in ApprovalRepository uses a status field as the source of truth; checking status before each transition reuses this pattern and prevents out-of-order advances

decision_log.decision: Send notifications to the next-step reviewer via the existing NotificationService after each successful advance
decision_log.rationale: Reviewers need a prompt so they know a document is waiting; the NotificationService already handles delivery retries

codebase_evidence.finding: ApprovalRepository fetches the approval record with a pessimistic lock; the status field is used as the canonical step gate for all transition guards
